back_translation: Ahaz was twenty years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem sixteen years. He did not do what was right in the eyes of the LORD, as his father David had done.
But he walked in the ways of the kings of Israel, and he even cast images for the Baals.
He burned incense in the Valley of Ben-hinnom and burned his sons in the fire, according to the abominations of the nations whom the LORD had driven out before the Israelites.
He sacrificed and burned incense on the high places, on the hills, and under every green tree.

Therefore the LORD his God delivered Ahaz into the hand of the king of Aram, who defeated him and took many captives to Damascus. He was also delivered into the hand of the king of Israel, who struck him with a heavy blow.
Pekah son of Remaliah killed 120,000 men in Judah in one day, all valiant men, because they had forsaken the LORD, the God of their fathers.
And Zichri, a warrior of Ephraim, killed Maaseiah the king’s son, Azrikam the commander of the palace, and Elkanah the second to the king.
The Israelites took 200,000 of their relatives captive—women, sons, and daughters—and also carried off much plunder, and brought it to Samaria.

But there was a prophet of the LORD there named Oded. He went out to meet the army returning to Samaria and said to them, “Behold, because the LORD, the God of your fathers, was angry with Judah, He gave them into your hand; but you have slaughtered them in a rage that reaches up to heaven.
And now you intend to oppress the men and women of Judah and Jerusalem as your slaves. But do you yourselves not have sins against the LORD your God?
Now listen to me and send back the captives you took from your relatives, for the fierce wrath of the LORD is upon you.”
Then some of the leaders of Ephraim—Azariah son of Jehohanan, Berechiah son of Meshillemoth, Jehizkiah son of Shallum, and Amasa son of Hadlai—rose up against those returning from the war.

They said to them, “Do not bring the captives in here, for you are about to bring guilt against the LORD upon us. You intend to add to our sins and our guilt, for our guilt is already great, and fierce wrath is upon Israel.”
So the armed men left the captives and the plunder before the leaders and all the assembly.

Then the men designated by name rose up and took the captives, and from the plunder they clothed all who were naked. They gave them clothing, sandals, food and water, anointed them with oil, set all the feeble on donkeys, and brought them to Jericho, the City of Palms, to their relatives. Then they returned to Samaria.
At that time King Ahaz sent to the king of Assyria for help.
For the Edomites had again come and attacked Judah and carried away captives.

And the Philistines had raided the cities of the lowlands and the Negev of Judah, and captured Beth-shemesh, Aijalon, Gederoth, Soco with its villages, Timnah with its villages, and Gimzo with its villages, and they settled there.
For the LORD humbled Judah because of Ahaz king of Israel, for he had let Judah go without restraint and had been very unfaithful to the LORD.
Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria came against him and brought him trouble instead of strengthening him.
For Ahaz took a portion from the house of the LORD, from the royal palace, and from the princes, and gave it to the king of Assyria, but it did not help him at all.
In the time of his distress, this King Ahaz became even more unfaithful to the LORD.

For he sacrificed to the gods of Damascus, who had defeated him, and said, “Because the gods of the kings of Aram helped them, I will sacrifice to them so they may help me.” But these gods became the ruin of him and of all Israel.
Ahaz gathered the vessels of the house of God, cut them into pieces, shut the doors of the house of the LORD, and made himself altars at every street corner in Jerusalem.
In every city of Judah he made high places to burn incense to other gods, provoking the LORD, the God of his fathers, to anger.
As for the rest of his acts and all his ways, from beginning to end, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel.
Ahaz rested with his fathers, and they buried him in the city, in Jerusalem, but they did not bring him into the tombs of the kings of Israel. And Hezekiah his son reigned in his place.
